level_signals: Described as 'lead product contributor shaping our Workflow Platform'
Highly visible, company-wide infrastructure domain with stakeholder management to leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Extensive product management experience, preferably dealing with developer-facing products, platform infrastructure, or enterprise SaaS.

You understand the difference between building an isolated application and building the foundational platform that powers an ecosystem. You excel at providing developers with the tools and services needed to build end-to-end processes.

You are comfortable navigating distributed systems, state machines, and durable execution platforms (experience with Temporal is a major plus). You can easily read and review technical requirements with engineering leads.

You bring a service-oriented approach to collaborating with cross-functional teams, aiming to stop other groups from reinventing workflow orchestration and creating duplication across the product suite.

You can take a complex web of dependencies across major company-wide initiatives—including data integrations, enterprise features, AI advancements, and client onboarding—and synthesise them into a clear, prioritised roadmap.
You can effectively communicate the status of complex technical initiatives to non-technical stakeholders and leadership.
Applicants must have, and maintain, the right to work in the United Kingdom from the first day of employment. Please note that visa sponsorship is not available for this role.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: experience with Temporal is a major plus
Experience working with financial data (custodians, transactions, positions, security masters) or enterprise security/data reconciliation processes is highly valued.
Extensive product management experience, preferably dealing with developer-facing products, platform infrastructure, or enterprise SaaS.

explicitly_excluded: Visa sponsorship candidates (explicitly stated: 'visa sponsorship is not available for this role')

scope.surface: Workflow Platform — foundational infrastructure orchestration layer powering client-defined automation, AI agents, and internal developer tools
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS (wealth management / investment data platform)
primary_metrics_inferred: Platform adoption by internal engineering teams (reduction of duplication)
core_responsibilities: Define the vision, roadmap, and execution for the Workflow Platform
Power client-defined automation without bespoke engineering for each use case
Build a reliable execution layer for AI agents performing long-running multi-step tasks
Balance immediate stability improvements with long-term strategic architectural changes
Define and expand a shared library of atomic, reusable workflow components
Leverage durable execution frameworks (like Temporal) to guarantee application completion
Lead expansion of Workflow Platform across international regions
Release intuitive workflow building capabilities to clients

green_flags: Highly specific product surface with clear ownership (Workflow Platform as foundational infrastructure)
Role is explicitly described as 'highly visible' with company-wide impact
Concrete example impact areas provided (AI Agent Execution, Task & Approval Workflows, Global Infrastructure)
Addepar manages nearly $9 trillion in assets — substantial scale
Clear technical context around durable execution frameworks (Temporal) signals engineering maturity
red_flags: No visa sponsorship available, limiting candidate pool to UK right-to-work holders only
'Extensive' experience required but no years defined — ambiguous seniority bar
Location details are vague — no specific UK city named for the role, only 'United Kingdom'
No mention of team size or direct reports structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Staff' title and 'extensive' experience language, not comp)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.type set to 'onsite' — JD does not mention remote or hybrid options; only specifies UK right to work
industries_named on financial_data nice-to-have (inferred from 'custodians, transactions, positions, security masters' context)
ambiguities: Location type is unclear — JD says 'right to work in the United Kingdom' but does not specify whether the role is onsite, hybrid, or remote within the UK. Treated as 'onsite' given no remote language is present.
No specific UK city is named for this role, though Addepar lists London and Edinburgh as offices. 'United Kingdom' used as cities_listed value.

The 'preferably dealing with developer-facing products, platform infrastructure, or enterprise SaaS' sentence is used both as a must-have (extensive PM experience) and extracted separately as a nice-to-have for the 'preferably' qualifier — this dual extraction reflects the sentence's mixed framing.
